acceptance_targets: [chapter_15_award_01] Pass if the revised scene between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:301 and the interruption at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:325 produces one concrete consequence of the sex that would still matter if the alerts were deleted. Fail if the scene still moves from emotional complication directly into corporate interruption without a new self-generated consequence.

[chapter_15_dialogue_01] Pass if the revised exchange starting at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:285 gives Nita at least one response grounded in an immediate object or bodily consequence in the room, and at least one of the current aphoristic summaries is replaced by interrupted, defensive, or self-correcting speech. Fail if both characters still get consecutive lines that read like settled retrospective theories of the marriage.

[chapter_15_prose_01] Pass if the revised chapter leaves no more than two instances of the cited emphatic templates, and the revised moments at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:103, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:181, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:299 now turn through fresh sensory or action detail instead of another 'That was...' or 'He looked at her' construction. Fail if three or more of those formulas remain.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised scene between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:301 and the interruption at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:325 produces one concrete consequence of the sex that would still matter if the alerts were deleted. Fail if the scene still moves from emotional complication directly into corporate interruption without a new self-generated consequence.
findings.evidence: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:311, Ben finally names the sink-mat gesture as proof that the relapse was not empty; by snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:325 the scene is interrupted by synchronized phone alerts, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:343 pivots both characters straight into showcase logistics.
findings.problem: The chapter's most dangerous morning-after beat gets cut off by a perfectly timed external intrusion. That keeps the plot moving, but at shortlist level it feels too convenient: the couple do not have to generate one more irrevocable consequence of the relapse on their own before the systems reclaim the room.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the exchange anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:301, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:311, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:325 so the relapse changes strategy or boundaries before the alerts arrive. Options: let the sink-mat beat force a named boundary, an altered sabotage posture, or a concrete decision about what they will or will not tell Mina or Leela; or delay the showcase email until after that consequence lands.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange starting at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:285 gives Nita at least one response grounded in an immediate object or bodily consequence in the room, and at least one of the current aphoristic summaries is replaced by interrupted, defensive, or self-correcting speech. Fail if both characters still get consecutive lines that read like settled retrospective theories of the marriage.
findings.evidence: The block at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:285, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:289, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:293, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:297 gives both Ben and Nita polished retrospective diagnoses of the marriage in consecutive beats.
findings.problem: The morning-after argument gets a little too thesis-clean for these two voices. Nita's style-bible profile specifically warns against polished self-summary, yet lines like her body 'lying for the whole rest of me' and the neat account of their recurring pattern sound more authorially shaped than sleep-starved, defensive, and stuck in this room should sound.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the exchange at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:289, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:293, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:297 by breaking at least one of Nita's summative lines against immediate physical detail, interruption, self-correction, or household pressure. Keep Ben's confession at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:285 under pressure too so the scene does not let both characters deliver clean interpretive monologues back-to-back.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter leaves no more than two instances of the cited emphatic templates, and the revised moments at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:103, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:181, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:299 now turn through fresh sensory or action detail instead of another 'That was...' or 'He looked at her' construction. Fail if three or more of those formulas remain.
findings.evidence: The narration reuses the same emphatic templates at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:35 ('That was the...'), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:103 ('That was the...'), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:181 ('That was what...'), plus the repeated 'He looked at her' turn at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:117, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:299, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:355.
findings.problem: By the third reuse, these sentence shapes stop feeling like choices and start feeling like defaults. In a chapter that otherwise depends on fresh bodily specificity, the repeated abstract-labeling move and repeated 'looked at her' beat flatten the prose into recognizable autopilot.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary or cut the repeated templates at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:35, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:103, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:117, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:181, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:299, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:355. Replace at least two of them with sensory or kinetic writing that lets the judgment emerge without another abstract label or stock looking beat.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the ugly overlap between office systems and intimate life: sweat under formalwear, sponsored groceries in the middle of a fight, sex that clarifies nothing, a child noticing coercion through object logic. When choosing between elegant abstraction and embarrassing specificity, choose specificity unless the character is actively lying to themselves. If a scene risks becoming pure farce, restore weight through money, care work, or the body's limits; if it risks becoming solemn prestige fiction, let the absurd machinery stay absurd.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a draft replaces bodily fact, financial shame, sexual consequence, or emotional cruelty with tasteful summary, rewrite until the cost is present in scene rather than merely reported.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the norm in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, ritual formality, or character-specific self-control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should move the same way. Follow spectacle with aftermath, ritual with drift, humiliation with quiet, revelation with preparation. Vary scene count, dialogue density, location spread, and whether the chapter advances by confrontation, seduction, dread, comic escalation, or changed understanding.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete contemporary English with room for corporate and systems language when characters would actually think or speak that way; every abstract term should be contaminated by bodily or household reality nearby.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third that stays inside the current focalizer's sensory field and only shifts focalization at scene breaks.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate dry compression for comic pressure with longer, accrual-based sentences when shame, fatigue, or desire flood the body. Let jokes land through timing and juxtaposition rather than narrator commentary.
